mapping.a_element: momentum probability distribution whose statistical structure is analyzed
mapping.b_element: random codeword ensemble / interfering-signal distribution being analyzed

mapping.a_element: statistical-mechanics framing of an ideal gas
mapping.b_element: statistical-physics analytical tools applied to the decoder

brief_justification: The only overlap is the broad 'statistical physics' umbrella: paper A's machinery is a definition of thermodynamic temperature tied to the Fisher information of the momentum distribution, whereas paper B's machinery is a large-deviation / partition-function derivation of error exponents for optimum decoding of an interference channel. These are different governing structures (an estimation-theoretic temperature definition vs. exponential-rate asymptotics of a codeword ensemble), so no coherent role-for-role mapping holds.
